query: What is the probability a 55-year-old with chest pain has PE?

questions.question: Does the patient have known risk factors (recent surgery, malignancy, immobility)?
questions.why: Risk factors swing pre-test probability dramatically.

questions.question: Is a D-dimer result available?
questions.why: D-dimer is the most common rule-out test in low pre-test probability presentations.

questions.question: What imaging is feasible (CTPA, V/Q, none)?
questions.why: Imaging modality determines downstream sensitivity and specificity.

components.name: Pre-test probability
components.description: Wells score derived pre-test probability of PE for this presentation.
components.why: Anchors the entire Bayesian update; small shifts cascade.

components.name: D-dimer sensitivity
components.description: Sensitivity of high-sensitivity ELISA D-dimer for PE in this age group.
components.why: Determines how strongly a negative D-dimer can rule out PE.

components.name: D-dimer specificity
components.description: Specificity is age-dependent and a known weak point of the test.

components.name: CTPA sensitivity
components.description: Sensitivity of modern multi-detector CTPA for segmental and larger PE.
components.why: Limits how confidently a negative CTPA closes the workup.

components.name: CTPA specificity
components.description: Specificity of modern CTPA; high but not perfect.
components.why: Determines false-positive rate driving unnecessary anticoagulation.
